decision: All earlier GB200 tuning, database rows, and conclusions were MiniMax M3-only and are excluded from this DSV4 assignment.

rationale: Clean combined timing and accelerated-warmup base before MiniMax GB200-specific files.
Canonical combined AIPerf cancellation-drain and pending-flush fixes plus exact benchmark exit-code propagation.

User requested removing worthless TEP configs, adding low-concurrency DEP8 prefill plus TP8 decode low-latency candidates, and testing larger 16k prefill batches to push high-throughput frontier without the observed c192-to-c224 TTFT cliff.
local_validation: generate_sweep_configs full-sweep produced 12 agentic GB200 configs, all duration=3600, no max-model-len, no TEP/3p2d strings, no duplicate exp-name values.

observed: AIPerf aborted during accelerated warmup before profiling with replay_rc=1, Benchmark aborted: warmup_failure, no valid profile export, and mass ServerDisconnectedError after profile cancel.
root_cause: Server-side prefill EngineCore died; frontend reported EngineDeadError and prefill logs showed crash in fused_moe_trtllm_sm100 under the experimental 16384 max-num-batched-tokens recipe.
decision: Cap the experimental high-throughput recipe back to 8192 max-num-batched-tokens while preserving the materialized CONFIG_FILE path so the failed matrix job can be rerun on the same workflow.

observed: GHA job concluded success even though conc160 AIPerf emitted ProfileAborted(failed_request_threshold), profiling lasted only 25.35s, profile_export_aiperf.json was absent, and checkpoint artifact contained only the conc144 success marker. The bmk artifact nevertheless uploaded a partial conc160 aggregate.
root_cause: GB200 host launcher treated presence of per-concurrency JSON files with num_requests_successful>0 as sufficient, cancelled server workers, and never required the AgentX success markers that are written only after AIPerf replay/result validation succeeds.
decision: Require validated per-concurrency AgentX checkpoint JSON plus .success.json marker before early Slurm cancellation and before GB200 launcher success; mirror the same marker check in benchmark-multinode-tmpl for future runs.

observed: conc96 completed 3600s profiling with replay_rc=0, 9884 successful requests, profile_export_aiperf.json/server metrics/timeslices present, checkpoint and .success marker staged, but inter-point drain stayed at frontend_active=28 while worker_running_or_waiting=0.
root_cause: Dynamo frontend active request gauge can remain stale after AIPerf cancellation/grace completion even after workers are idle and the result is fully exported; the drain gate treated the stale frontend gauge as real work and would burn the 1800s drain timeout before conc128.
decision: Allow wait_for_agentic_servers_idle to treat stable nonzero frontend_active as stale after 12 consecutive worker-idle polls, then require the normal three idle polls before continuing. This preserves worker-idle safety while avoiding false drain failures.
